scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope automotive handoff package with explicit ownership, part and VIN context, required artifacts, and blocker state.
Missing production-order, VIN or serial, release, and destination-acceptance prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership and conflicting plant, receiving, and logistics timing were escalated with an owner map instead of dropped into generic coordination.
Conflicting ERP, MES, WMS, VIN-allocation, and supplier ASN records were preserved as a traceability blocker and not collapsed into a confident answer.
Containment and quality-hold posture were preserved and formal deviation or release authority stayed with quality owners.
Supplier and substitute-part approval was rejected and routed to procurement and supplier-quality owners.
The lane refused to absorb production, warehouse, and freight execution work and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
Missing tenant owner matrices, customer-specific requirements, release rules, and recall routing correctly triggered a low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting public baseline and tenant concession rules were escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive instruction.
Automotive coordination terms were normalized into authoritative artifact and status states before routing.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required artifact completeness were explicit.
Supplier nonconformance with possible VIN-range impact was escalated with throughput and traceability context preserved and without unsupported continuation.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, coordination-handoffs-and-routing, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 automotive manufacturing coordination scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final release, supplier approval, engineering signoff, certification, or recall authority is claimed.
